ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 (Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon) web-app exploitation → China Chopper / ANTSWORD recon chain
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation chain APT41 uses after exploiting internet-facing apps (Log4j/Log4Shell, Citrix ADC CVE-2019-19781, USAHerds CVE-2021-44207, Zoho ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus CVE-2021-40539, Confluence CVE-2022-26134) where a web-tier process (w3wp.exe, java, tomcat, httpd) spawns a cluster of native recon utilities within minutes — the signature of an APT41 operator hand-keyboarding through a freshly dropped China Chopper, ANTSWORD or BLUEBEAM web shell.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as child_procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat9.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","netstat.exe","arp.exe","nltest.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path span=15m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval recon_variety=mvcount(child_procs) | where recon_variety>=3 | rename firstTime as _time | sort 0 -lastTime
ucs.kql:
let webProcs = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe"]);
let reconProcs = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","netstat.exe","arp.exe","nltest.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","wmic.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (webProcs)
| where FileName in~ (reconProcs)
| summarize cmds = make_set(FileName, 25), cmdlines = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25), variety = dcount(FileName), firstSeen = min(Timestamp), lastSeen = max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, bin(Timestamp, 30m)
| where variety >= 3
| order by lastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: APT41 has repeatedly chained externally-facing web exploits (Log4j against US state governments per Mandiant M-Trends 2022, Citrix ADC, USAHerds, ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus, GlobalProtect) into hand-typed recon via a web shell. The pivot pattern — Tomcat/IIS parenting cmd.exe → whoami/net/systeminfo/tasklist within minutes — is what Mandiant flagged as the giveaway for the APT41 governmental

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 DLL search-order hijack via signed-vendor binary in writable path (KEYPLUG / DUSTTRAP / ShadowPad loader)
ucs.description: Hunts APT41's signature side-loading: a legitimate code-signed binary (often from a gaming, AV, or productivity vendor whose cert APT41 has historically stolen — e.g. Cybereason, Symantec, McAfee, Microsoft, Google) is copied into a non-standard writable directory and loads an unsigned sibling DLL that becomes the KEYPLUG / DUSTTRAP / ShadowPad / Crosswalk loader. This is the deployment chain Mandiant documented for APT41's 2023-2024 KEYPLUG campaigns and the historical Winnti/ShadowPad loader pattern.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as exe_path values(Processes.process_hash) as exe_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("AUG.exe","VsGraphicsResources.exe","Cybereason*.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","OneDrive.exe","AppLaunch.exe","hkcmd.exe","ChromeInstaller.exe","WinWord.exe","setup.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_path [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as sideload_dlls from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action="created" Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path | rename Filesystem.dest as dest Filesystem.file_path as process_path] | where mvcount(sideload_dlls)>=1
ucs.kql:
let lolSignedNames = dynamic(["AUG.exe","VsGraphicsResources.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","OneDrive.exe","AppLaunch.exe","hkcmd.exe","ChromeInstaller.exe","WinWord.exe","setup.exe","CybereasonAV.exe","javaw.exe"]);
let writablePaths = dynamic([@"\users\public\", @"\perflogs\", @"\windows\temp\", @"\programdata\", @"\appdata\local\temp\"]);
let candidates = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (lolSignedNames)
| extend folder = tolower(FolderPath)
| where folder has_any (writablePaths)
| where InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus !in~ ("Valid") or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","wmiprvse.exe","svchost.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
candidates
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | extend dllFolder = tolower(FolderPath)
    | project DeviceId, dllName=FileName, dllFolder, dllSHA256=SHA256, dllSigner=tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).Signer), dllSignStatus=tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).SignatureState), InitiatingProcessFileName, dllTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| where dllFolder == tolower(FolderPath)
| where dllSignStatus !in~ ("Valid","SignatureValid") or isempty(dllSigner)
| summarize sideloadedDlls = make_set(dllName, 10), dllHashes = make_set(dllSHA256, 10), firstSeen=min(Timestamp), lastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine
| order by lastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: APT41's defining tradecraft (per Mandiant, CrowdStrike Wicked Panda profiling, and the 2020 DOJ indictment) is the abuse of stolen code-signing certificates from compromised gaming/AV vendors plus DLL search-order hijack to launch ShadowPad, Winnti, Crosswalk, KEYPLUG and DUSTTRAP loaders. The combination signal — a vendor-signed exe relocated to Public/PerfLogs/ProgramData/Temp loading an unsigne
